mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and decelerating inflation indicates resilient consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline when RSAFS rises and CPIAUCSL falls, with forward returns negative in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during post-shock recovery phases reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns in recovery regimes, with 60-day forward returns negative in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing below 1.4 million units signals collapsing construction demand and broader economic contraction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods when HOUST drops below the threshold, or Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices crossing above 320 combined with restrictive policy rates force central banks to maintain tight liquidity, compressing equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate environment yields positive forward returns in >60% of cases, invalidating the valuation compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with negative outcomes occurring in >55% of periods or rolling Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth crossing below 1.5% alongside contracting industrial output indicates systemic supply-side stagnation, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when both GDPC1 and INDPRO signal weakness, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance.
